Introduction 1.1 Background The HFC AWTP is permitted to treat 96 mgd of raw sewage with a Type I two-stage, high rate (pure oxygen and conventional fine bubble aeration) activated sludge biological domestic wastewater treatment plant. - [Project Identification / Executive Summary] [Evaluation Memo] Report Creator Software Introduction The Howard F. Curren Advanced Water Treatment Plant(HFC AWTP) owns and operates a comprehensive Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition(SCADA) system. This system is primarily composed of General Electric(GE) PAC System Rx3i and 90-30 series programmable logic controllers(PLCs) at the field level, and VTScada by Trihedral at the SCADA/HMI level. SCADA systems are critical for monitoring and controlling industrial processes, providing real-time data, and enabling remote control and automation of plant operations. As part of ongoing improvement efforts, the City is currently in the process of upgrading t... (26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf)

A dead-band setting is applied to all limit alarms monitoring a process value. The configuration parameter is applicable to analog alarms only. The amount is supplied in engineering units with a value of zero deactivating the feature. Alarms are currently all set to have debounce times at zero. (26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf)

### Project Overview
**Project Overview: SCADA Migration and System Upgrade for City of Tampa (HFC AWTP)**

The primary objective of this project is to migrate remaining facilities operating on legacy HSQ software at the Howard F. Curren Advanced Water Treatment Plant (AWTP) entirely to VTScada, ensuring continuous operation throughout the transition [RFQ]. The plant currently treats 96 mgd of raw sewage using a Type I two-stage activated sludge system and discharges effluent to Tampa Bay; residuals are anaerobically digested and dewatered for land application as Class B residual [Background Reference]. This initiative aligns with recently completed SCADA and Process Control System (PCS) programming, configuration, and design standards established by the City [RFQ; Workshop Note], ensuring compliance while upgrading aging infrastructure installed decades ago.

**System Migration and Data Integrity**
*   **Software Transition:** The project involves documenting current network diagrams and control system programming to facilitate a seamless shift from HSQ to VTScada across all facilities. Historical data must be saved, transferred starting at least from the beginning of 2000, and made available within the new environment [Background Reference].
*   **Development Participation:** To ensure familiarity for future troubleshooting, City staff will participate directly in graphics development, tag creation, and programming during construction phases. This includes oversight of HMI screen and PLC control development via individual review meetings prior to finalizing controls [Workshop Note] [RFQ]. The design team coordinates with process integrators to develop these elements while maintaining continuous plant operation [Background Reference].

**Hardware and Network Infrastructure**
*   **Fiber Optic Replacement:** A key scope item involves evaluating alternatives for replacing the fiber optic cable network. While new cables can be installed using MaxCell sleeves in existing conduits where space is limited, replacement must occur without interrupting current network functions to maintain service continuity [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf]. During installation of new cables, existing ones shall remain in place pending field investigations to verify infrastructure compatibility [Background Reference] [Workshop Note].
*   **Network Equipment:** All network equipment is required to be new, replacing assets with 100mb/s speed capabilities located in the current operations area or a future building. Site investigations confirmed that while eleven main trunk fiber cables are landed at a patch panel in the Main Pump Station, PLC 19 connects directly via CAT6 cable without using fiber [RFQ; Background Reference].
*   **Redundancy and Safety:** Proposed revised fiber paths for redundancy will be detailed in the basis of design report following workshop discussions on hardware configuration. Loss of current PLCs would shut down critical permit analyzers, chemical feeds, and samplers (STAR), necessitating robust safety measures [Workshop Note].

**Standards and Compliance Requirements**
*   **Design Standards:** The project must adhere to City-established standards for SCADA/PCS programming and design. Appendix B outlines specific forms required for deviations or changes to these standards [RFQ] [SCADA Standard]. Workshop notes from October 5, 2021, established initial design criteria for P&IDs and PLC/HMI software development to guide these improvements [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf].
*   **HMI Specifications:** Graphics development is decided during SCADA workshops; dewatering displays will set standards for all processes prior to startup (anticipated spring 2023) [Background Reference]. HMI tag names must include the "HFC" prefix, and unique ISA instrument designations specific to the City are utilized. Security considerations are central, with workshops establishing standards for internet/site security protection alongside P&ID formats and network improvements [Workshop Note; Evaluation Memo].
*   **P&ID Formats:** The format used for dewatering project P&IDs will be applied generally. Hazen proposed using Smart P&IDs which offer benefits discussed in workshop meetings regarding communication paths and hardware configuration [Workshop Note] [Background Reference]. Specific platform costs (e.g., Ignition non-cloud edition) were factored into scalability and pricing rankings during the selection process, though not all cost details are fully indicated in initial quotes [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf].

**Reporting Software Evaluation**
The City has tasked a consultant with reviewing report creation software to ensure seamless integration with existing database systems for regulatory compliance and performance monitoring. This evaluation aims to select new software capable of generating plant operating reports required by regulations, distinct from the core SCADA migration tasks [Evaluation Memo] [RFQ].

**Submission Logistics**
Submissions related to this RFQ (including Standard Form SF330, "Ban-The-Box" compliance, and SLBE solicitation forms) must be emailed to ContractAdministration@tampagov.net before 2 P.M. on March 19, 2026 [RFQ].

### Procurement And Commercial Requirements
**Procurement And Commercial Requirements Summary**

The City of Tampa's Request for Qualifications (RFQ) for the Howard F. Curren Advanced Water Treatment Plant (HFC AWTP) SCADA migration project establishes specific submission protocols, legal compliance mandates, and commercial constraints distinct from technical evaluation criteria or design standards. All proposals must be submitted as a PDF file via email to `ContractAdministration@tampagov.net` no later than 2:00 P.M. on March 19, 2026; however, the City does not guarantee acknowledgement for same-day submissions received by this deadline [RFQ]. Firms are responsible for confirming receipt of their proposals and must adhere to Florida Statute Section 287.055(10) regarding proposal reuse, as well as exemptions concerning internal building plans per Sections 119.071(3), 119.07(1), and Article I, Section 24(a) of the Florida Constitution [RFQ].

**Mandatory Forms and Good Faith Efforts (GFE)**
To avoid being deemed non-responsive, firms must complete, sign, and submit specific forms: DMI Form 10 (Solicited Subcontractors), DMI Form 20 (Utilized Subcontractors), and DMI Form 50 (Good Faith Effort Compliance Plan) [RFQ]. Failure to provide these renders the bid non-responsive. The GFE plan must document outreach efforts via email, fax, letters, or phone calls for all firms on the SLBE Goal Setting List. Proof of certification from Small Local Business Enterprises (SLBE) is required alongside these forms. All subcontractors projected to be utilized must be included in DMI Form 20 regardless of their ownership structure or size, while solicited and unsolicited quotation recipients are listed on DMI Form 10. The prime contractor remains responsible for Good Faith Efforts even if they possess the ability to perform work themselves; other subcontracting opportunities should still be explored to meet participation goals [Other Source].

**Evaluation Criteria Distinction and Technical Scope**
While technical requirements such as PLC hardware longevity, VT SCADA compatibility, software cost competitiveness, and customer service accessibility are detailed in Evaluation Memos, these represent scoring criteria rather than binding RFQ submission constraints. Similarly, documentation standards for change management (e.g., date codes, programmer initials) found in the SCADA Standards Volume 2 apply to project execution but do not constitute immediate procurement requirements unless explicitly referenced as such [Evaluation Memo].

The commercial landscape centers on migrating monitoring capabilities to VTScada software while adhering to specific network diagrams, control system programming needs, and regulatory reporting requirements. The City aims to select a new report creation platform that integrates seamlessly with existing databases to ensure efficient plant performance identification and compliance. Evaluation memos highlight significant cost disparities among platforms: the PI System is identified as the highest-cost option ($174k/year) due to its licensing structure, whereas XLReport offers a lower recurring subscription model (~$5,850/year). Licensing constraints are noted as restrictive for some options regarding tag and user counts. Technical standards mandate that source protection (locking proprietary code) be prohibited, access to network hosts require authenticated encrypted connections, and remote sites must be polled from central locations without direct database interaction by operators [Evaluation Memo].

### Scope of Work: Technical Standards, System Architecture, Hardware Specifications, and Procedural Requirements

The project scope for the City of Tampa's migration from legacy HSQ software to VTScada at facilities such as the Howard F. Curren Advanced Wastewater Treatment Plant (HFC AWTP) establishes a comprehensive framework integrating established SCADA standards with specific project constraints [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf]. Managed by Hazen and Sawyer under City oversight, the work encompasses documenting network diagrams in Microsoft Visio/PDF, programming control systems using Studio 5000 Logix Designer (Version 36 or latest), selecting new hardware that meets regulatory needs, evaluating fiber optic replacement alternatives with cost estimates, preparing signed construction plans [RFQ], and preserving historical HSQ data from January 2000 onward within the system.

**System Architecture and Communications**
The design team must coordinate closely with process integrators and City staff for VTScada software development, graphics creation, tag programming, and troubleshooting training. The data acquisition infrastructure mandates adherence to strict redundancy requirements; specifically, at least two dedicated data acquisition servers configured as primary/backup pairs are required for collections/distributions, wastewater operations, and water operations, while separate stand-alone servers are mandated for these roles to ensure system resilience [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf], [SCADA Standard]. An industrial DMZ acts as the sole communication buffer between business networks and PCS/SCADA systems. Architectures utilizing dual-homed hosts, dial-up modems, cellular routers, or remote network connections are prohibited; updates must be performed locally on a City-provided laptop to ensure consistent software versioning [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf], [SCADA Standard]. Controllers must utilize non-volatile memory (NVM) capable of withstanding environmental conditions without relying on continuous power, while all communications are configured in VTScada using appropriate drivers to match specific protocols. The existing surveillance equipment inventory includes specific Sony Ipela models deployed in chlorine rooms, railcars, switchgear areas, and gates, complemented by Cambium Canopy devices utilized for railcar transmission [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf].

**Design Criteria & Documentation Deliverables**
Process and Instrumentation Diagrams (P&IDs) must be developed using AutoCAD Plant 3D, 2022, or Smart P&ID software to ensure accurate I/O lists. Tagging protocols distinguish between process equipment and instrumentation tags, with legends provided in Appendix C of relevant documentation [Background Reference], [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf]. Key decisions include assigning building numbers 054 (Sludge Storage Tanks Control Building), 058 (Blower Building), and new structures as 065/066 (*Workshop Note*) [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf].

**Programming Logic, Languages, and Nomenclature**
Development must utilize Ladder Diagrams (LD) or Function Block Diagrams (FBD); other languages like Instruction List are not permitted without a formal deviation request [SCADA Standard], [Background Reference]. LD is preferred for complex Boolean logic, interlocks, sequencing, and I/O control. While function blocks may be used within rung logic, the system enforces a strict contract regarding data types (Booleans, integers, reals) accessible via AOI interfaces using EasyBuilder Pro V6 with specific function blocks defined for Modbus communication: `RAC_OPRNETMODBUSTCPMASTER` for sending data and `RAC_OPRNETMODBUSTCPCLIENT`.

### Controls And Integration Requirements
**Controls and Integration Requirements Summary**

The City of Tampa's SCADA and Process Control System (PCS) framework establishes strict controls for change management, programming languages, security, hardware integration, and network architecture to ensure consistent reliability across all plants. The system design mandates a robust VTScada 12.1.58 architecture from Trihedral Engineering as the foundation for all new HMI screen development; consequently, all graphical elements must be created using the latest available City-provided ChangeSet file to ensure application recreation capability [SCADA Standard]. Redundancy is achieved through at least two dedicated, stand-alone servers configured as primary and backup roles specifically for data acquisition across collections/distributions, waste water operations, and water operations.

**Network Architecture and Security Zones**
The system architecture mandates strict network segmentation to ensure security and autonomy. An industrial DMZ must act as a buffer between business networks (e.g., PIN) and PCS/SCADA systems, serving as the sole communication bridge; dual-homed hosts are prohibited [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf]. Network architecture must maintain disconnection points between security zones to allow for isolation while preserving limited operational continuity. Remote sites shall operate autonomously without dependency on other plant PCs. Authentication is centrally managed via domain-level services (e.g., Active Directory), with local authentication explicitly noted where used exclusively, and a dedicated solution required for the SCADA system [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf]. Physical access controls require keyed locks for control rooms/server areas, disabling unused router/switch ports except during configuration/testing. Remote desktop protocols (RDP) or third-party tools lacking domain authentication, accounting, encryption, and strong security features are prohibited. Dial-up modems and cellular routers are also restricted [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf].

**Communication Protocols and Hardware Integration**
Control I/O signals interfacing with vendor-supplied PLCs must be hardwired, distinct from the overall system PLCs provided by the integrator. Communication protocols between remote controllers and the supervisory system shall strictly adhere to DNP3 (as specified by DNP.ORG) on remote sites, utilizing appropriate transport layers like LAN or serial modems while prohibiting proprietary protocols; polling must be event-driven rather than static value requests where possible [SCADA Standard]. Hardware specifications require industrial-grade HMI displays capable of withstanding harsh environments, connected via shielded CAT6 cable rated for 480V exposure where applicable. Power resilience is addressed through the use of APC UPS units without direct ethernet connections and equipped with bypass switches to facilitate easy changeouts; relays must be installed on UPS outputs to trigger alarms when panels operate solely on backup power [Background Reference]. Regarding equipment selection for PLC upgrades at the HFC AWTP, industry reviews identify GE (RX3i series), Rockwell (Allen Bradley Logix series), and Schneider (Modicon M540/580) as viable options with significant municipal wastewater market share. While these manufacturers were selected for further evaluation in an Evaluation Memo [Evaluation Memo], the specific decision to select one over another remains distinct from binding SCADA standards which focus on software compatibility at this stage. Field investigations have verified routing details noting that PLC 19 in the Main Pump Station connects directly via CAT6 cable rather than fiber, while eleven main trunk fiber cables are landed at a patch panel for process connections [Other Source/Field Investigation].

**Programming Logic and Development Environment**
PLC programming is mandated to use Studio 5000 Logix Designer Version 36 or later; the main routine must govern program flow using LD language (or FBDs) for Boolean equations, jump subroutines (

### Network And Electrical Infrastructure
**Network and Electrical Infrastructure Summary**

The project involves a SCADA migration and fiber replacement design for the Howard F. Curren Advanced Wastewater Treatment Plant (HFC AWTP), governed by specific hardware, network routing, and HMI standards while adhering to Florida public records exemptions regarding building plans [RFQ]. The Network and Electrical Infrastructure category addresses legacy hardware risks, data historian architecture, security patching protocols, and troubleshooting display methodologies within a SCADA environment. Specific evaluation memos highlight the presence of mixed PLC architectures across various buildings: Building 10 utilizes GE 90-30 legacy systems for RIO units 1 and 2 as well as unit 43, while Buildings 5, 59, 78, 30, and 25 employ active GE PACSystems Rx3i controllers (PLC-4, PLC-37, PLC-39, PLC-33, and PLC-40 respectively), with Building 25 also containing legacy RIO units for the Alkalinity process [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf].

**Hardware and Power Requirements:**
*   **PLC Architecture:** The system utilizes independent vendor PLCs for individual equipment systems that interface with integrator-provided overall system PLCs. Signals must be hardwired for all control I/O, and new PLCs are permitted to connect directly to the plant fiber network without remote telemetry units [Background Reference].
*   **Power Supply:** Uninterruptible power supplies (UPS) shall specifically use APC models that lack ethernet connections but include a bypass switch for easy change-out. Relays on UPS outputs must provide alarms when panels operate on backup power, and spare capacity requirements are set at 20% total with 10% installed [Background Reference].
*   **Standard Components:** Control panel components follow specific model standards (e.g., GE Rx3i CPUs) to ensure consistency across the application.

**Network Infrastructure and Routing:**
*   **Fiber vs. Copper Verification:** Field investigations confirmed that while fiber is used for main trunk cables landed at patch panels in the Main Pump Station, PLC 19 connects directly via CAT6 cable without using fiber. Existing infrastructure includes a conduit beneath the network room with limited space; replacing old fibers requires parallel installation to maintain functionality [Background Reference].
*   **Network Topology:** The facility utilizes distinct networks (e.g., Base 7 PC39328, COT Network) color-coded and zoned for specific functions like camera monitoring and server rooms. PLCs such as PLC 1, 9, and 18 act as critical hubs directing communications back to the main switch; their failure impacts connected units [Background Reference].
*   **Installation Constraints:** During new fiber installation, existing cables must remain in place. Field investigations are recommended during design phases to verify that new fibers can be installed within the existing infrastructure without interruption [Background Reference].

**Data Historian and Security Standards:**
Regarding infrastructure standards, SCADA Volume 3 mandates that historian data resides on a SQL-based server located in the PIN(DMZ) network to receive critical compliance information from acquisition servers; notably, there are no established City-wide standards for analog value logging frequency or deadbands, requiring project-specific review and approval [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf]. Security requirements outlined in SCADA Volume 1 dictate the maintenance of a patch management system within the PIN to ensure production operating systems receive vendor updates via an automated method using the City's business network and internet connection, alongside anti-virus definition updates for PCS/SCADA systems [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf].

### Deliverables And Commissioning
**Deliverables And Commissioning Summary**

The project scope requires migrating monitoring and control capabilities to VTScada software, which includes documenting SCADA network diagrams and control system programming [RFQ]. The firm must assist in selecting a new software system capable of generating plant operating reports for regulatory compliance and performance monitoring [RFQ], while also developing alternatives for replacing the fiber optic cable network with signed and sealed construction plans and specifications [RFQ].

**Hardware and System Standards:**
*   **Workstations:** Computer workstations shall be provided in key buildings to allow full control and monitoring of the entire plant from each location; existing stations are not to be replaced. All programmers must adhere to unified hardware and software standards, with independent vendor PLCs interfacing via hardwired signals for all I/O [Background Reference].
*   **Documentation Requirements:** Implementers are responsible for updating City SCADA & PCS documentation following changes, specifically including Process Control Descriptions (PCD), alarm/historian databases, P&IDs, loop diagrams, panel drawings, standard object libraries, reports, historian change logs, and network diagrams [SCADA Standard].
*   **Logic and Graphics Standards:** Ladder logic rungs must include comments describing their purpose; the first rung of a routine should summarize overall function. Digital values in HMI graphics must be shown in bold blue with simple descriptions within rectangles, consistent with Section 8.0 on alarming iconography [SCADA Standard].
*   **Task and File Organization:** FBD sheets are limited to sizes printable on an 11" x 17" sheet. Only two task types—continuous and periodic—are permitted for the City system; other types will not be employed [SCADA Standard].

**Specific Deliverable Constraints (Workshop Notes):**
*   **Instrument Tagging:** P&ID instrument tags must use standard ISA format bubbles but incorporate building letter codes alongside numbers to designate process areas. This specific tagging convention was noted as missing on previous dewater project P&IDs, requiring updates for those sheets [Workshop Note].

**Legal and Administrative Context (RFQ):**
*   The RFQ is subject to Florida Statutes Section 287.055(10) regarding reuse of information. Firms must confirm familiarity with exemptions under Sections 119.071(3) and relevant constitutional articles concerning building plans, blueprints, and schematic drawings that depict internal layouts or structural elements [RFQ].

I of the Florida Constitution concerning "Exempt Plans," as detailed in the RFQ Transmittal Memorandum for Project 26-D-00017 (HFC AWTP SCADA Migration). ### Risks And Clarifications
**Risks and Clarifications Summary**

The project involves specific cybersecurity risks and operational constraints regarding software selection, firmware updates, and network policies. DreamReport is highlighted for its advantages in NIST certification for cybersecurity and 21 CFR part 11 compliance, which ensures report reliability and integration capabilities with HMI/PLC systems [Evaluation Memo]. However, strict adherence to City policy is required; all devices must be managed by these policies, unencrypted communication (e.g., email) faces restrictions, and personnel must acknowledge cybersecurity requirements before accessing the PCS/SCADA system [SCADA Standard].

Operational risks are mitigated through rigorous update procedures. Developers or responsible parties must obtain written OT contact approval for any firmware updates requiring software changes and verify hardware compatibility against manufacturer matrices prior to updating; non-compatible components require specific handling per the Request for Shutdown Form [SCADA Standard]. Additionally, new software requirements necessitate a minimum four-week lead time for security evaluation and loading by the developer before startup begins [SCADA Standard].

Clarifications regarding design criteria were addressed in workshop notes. For instance, equipment tagging formats on revised P&IDs required correction to match specific building number conventions (e.g., XXXYYY ZZZ-AAA-BB), distinguishing these open items from binding standards or RFQ requirements [Workshop Note]. While tag naming codes exist for site locations and instruments, the documentation acknowledges that unaddressed needs may require submitting suggested additions to the City for consideration and approval before use [SCADA Standard].

### Executive Summary
**Executive Summary: SCADA Migration Project (HFC AWTP)**

The City of Tampa is initiating a comprehensive migration at the Howard F. Curren Advanced Water Treatment Plant (AWTP) to transition from legacy HSQ software to VTScada, ensuring continuous operation for its 96 mgd sewage treatment facility while upgrading decades-old infrastructure [RFQ]. Managed by Hazen and Sawyer under City oversight, this initiative aligns with established SCADA standards and addresses specific procurement protocols required by Florida Statute.

**Technical Scope and Architecture**
The project mandates a robust VTScada 12.1.58 architecture featuring redundant primary/backup data acquisition servers for all operations [SCADA Standard]. System design must strictly adhere to network segmentation policies, utilizing an industrial DMZ as the sole bridge between business networks (PIN) and PCS systems while prohibiting dual-homed hosts [RFQ; SCADA Standard]. Technical deliverables include documenting legacy GE PLC architectures (including 90-30 and PACSystems Rx3i units), preserving historical data from January 2000, and developing fiber optic replacement alternatives with signed construction plans [Background Reference; RFQ]. All hardware must interface via hardwired signals for control I/O, utilizing existing workstations where possible to maintain unified standards [Deliverables And Commissioning Summary].

**Compliance, Security, and Procurement Constraints**
Firms are subject to strict commercial constraints distinct from technical evaluation criteria. Proposals must be submitted as PDFs by March 19, 2026, accompanied mandatory Good Faith Effort (GFE) forms documenting subcontractor outreach [RFQ]. Cybersecurity is a critical risk mitigation factor; all devices require management under City policy with written OT contact approval for firmware updates and a minimum four-week lead time for security evaluation before startup begins [Risks And Clarifications Summary]. Additionally, the project leverages DreamReport software advantages regarding NIST certification and 21 CFR part 11 compliance to ensure report reliability [Evaluation Memo], while adhering to Florida public records exemptions concerning internal building plans [RFQ].

### Key Findings
* **Mandatory Submission Requirements:** Proposers must email a PDF submission containing SF330 forms, "Ban-The-Box" compliance data, SLBE utilization documentation (DMI 10,20,& 50), and the amount of City Work to ContractAdministration@tampagov.net before 2 P.M. on March 19, 2026; late submissions may not receive acknowledgement [Source: Procurement And Commercial Requirements].
* **Software Migration & Reporting Scope:** The project requires migrating monitoring/control capabilities to VTScada software and implementing a new system capable of generating regulatory plant operating reports; existing computer workstations will remain in place while independent vendor PLCs interface with integrator-provided overall system PLCs [Source: Deliverables And Commissioning].
* **Programming Language Constraints:** All control programming must strictly adhere to IEC 61131-3 standards, utilizing only Ladder Logic (LD) and Function Block Diagram (FBD); other languages such as Instruction List or Sequential Function Flowchart are not allowable [Source: Risks And Clarifications].
* **Cybersecurity & Compliance Standards:** New software must be submitted to the City with a minimum four-week lead time for security evaluation, and preferred solutions like DreamReport offer NIST certification which supports 21 CFR part 11 compliance requirements; all hardware/software programmers must follow unified standards [Source: Risks And Clarifications].
* **HMI Design Guidelines:** All new HMI objects and displays must strictly follow ISA-101 recommendations to maintain alignment with the City's Standard Object Library for effective process automation applications [Source: Network And Electrical Infrastructure].
* **Naming Convention Protocol:** A one-to-one relationship between controller tags and HMI visualized elements is required, ensuring tag names in the HMI are identical to those originating or terminating in the device per SCADA & PCS Standards Volume 2 [Source: Qualifications And Staffing].

* **Prepare Good Faith Effort (GFE) Documentation:** Compile proof of certification and completed DMI 10, 20, & 50 Forms to document inclusion of City-certified SLBE firms in all Phase 1 preconstruction design services. *(Source: Category: Scope Of Work)*
* **Review Hardware Standards for PLCs:** Ensure independent vendor PLC hardware plans adhere to the requirement that signals to controls are hardwired and that workstations allow full plant monitoring without replacing existing units. *(Source: Category: Controls And Integration Requirements)*
* **Validate HMI Design against ISA 101:** Confirm all new objects, displays, and graphics symbols in the proposed system strictly follow current ISA 101 recommendations to maintain industry standards. *(Source: Category: Network And Electrical Infrastructure)*
* **Check Software Security Compliance:** Verify that any proposed new software (e.g., DreamReport) is NIST certified for cybersecurity and capable of meeting 21 CFR part 11 compliance requirements before submission. *(Source: Category: Risks And Clarifications)*
* **Confirm Programming Language Usage:** Ensure all control system programming utilizes only Ladder Logic (LD) or Function Block Diagram (FBD), excluding Instruction List, Sequential Function Flowchart, and other non-approved languages per IEC 61131-3. *(Source: Category: Risks And Clarifications)*
* **Plan Software Security Evaluation Timeline:** Schedule a minimum of four weeks lead time to submit new software requirements for City security evaluation and loading prior to start-up. *(Source: Category: Risks And Clarifications)*

### Project Overview
**Project Overview: SCADA Migration and System Upgrade for City of Tampa (HFC AWTP)**

The primary objective of this project is to migrate remaining facilities operating on legacy HSQ software at the Howard F. Curren Advanced Water Treatment Plant (AWTP) entirely to VTScada, ensuring continuous operation throughout the transition [RFQ]. The plant currently treats 96 mgd of raw sewage using a Type I two-stage activated sludge system and discharges effluent to Tampa Bay; residuals are anaerobically digested and dewatered for land application as Class B residual [Background Reference]. This initiative aligns with recently completed SCADA and Process Control System (PCS) programming, configuration, and design standards established by the City [RFQ; Workshop Note], ensuring compliance while upgrading aging infrastructure installed decades ago.

**System Migration and Data Integrity**
*   **Software Transition:** The project involves documenting current network diagrams and control system programming to facilitate a seamless shift from HSQ to VTScada across all facilities. Historical data must be saved, transferred starting at least from the beginning of 2000, and made available within the new environment [Background Reference].
*   **Development Participation:** To ensure familiarity for future troubleshooting, City staff will participate directly in graphics development, tag creation, and programming during construction phases. This includes oversight of HMI screen and PLC control development via individual review meetings prior to finalizing controls [Workshop Note] [RFQ]. The design team coordinates with process integrators to develop these elements while maintaining continuous plant operation [Background Reference].

**Hardware and Network Infrastructure**
*   **Fiber Optic Replacement:** A key scope item involves evaluating alternatives for replacing the fiber optic cable network. While new cables can be installed using MaxCell sleeves in existing conduits where space is limited, replacement must occur without interrupting current network functions to maintain service continuity [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf]. During installation of new cables, existing ones shall remain in place pending field investigations to verify infrastructure compatibility [Background Reference] [Workshop Note].
*   **Network Equipment:** All network equipment is required to be new, replacing assets with 100mb/s speed capabilities located in the current operations area or a future building. Site investigations confirmed that while eleven main trunk fiber cables are landed at a patch panel in the Main Pump Station, PLC 19 connects directly via CAT6 cable without using fiber [RFQ; Background Reference].
*   **Redundancy and Safety:** Proposed revised fiber paths for redundancy will be detailed in the basis of design report following workshop discussions on hardware configuration. Loss of current PLCs would shut down critical permit analyzers, chemical feeds, and samplers (STAR), necessitating robust safety measures [Workshop Note].

**Standards and Compliance Requirements**
*   **Design Standards:** The project must adhere to City-established standards for SCADA/PCS programming and design. Appendix B outlines specific forms required for deviations or changes to these standards [RFQ] [SCADA Standard]. Workshop notes from October 5, 2021, established initial design criteria for P&IDs and PLC/HMI software development to guide these improvements [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf].
*   **HMI Specifications:** Graphics development is decided during SCADA workshops; dewatering displays will set standards for all processes prior to startup (anticipated spring 2023) [Background Reference]. HMI tag names must include the "HFC" prefix, and unique ISA instrument designations specific to the City are utilized. Security considerations are central, with workshops establishing standards for internet/site security protection alongside P&ID formats and network improvements [Workshop Note; Evaluation Memo].
*   **P&ID Formats:** The format used for dewatering project P&IDs will be applied generally. Hazen proposed using Smart P&IDs which offer benefits discussed in workshop meetings regarding communication paths and hardware configuration [Workshop Note] [Background Reference]. Specific platform costs (e.g., Ignition non-cloud edition) were factored into scalability and pricing rankings during the selection process, though not all cost details are fully indicated in initial quotes [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf].

**Reporting Software Evaluation**
The City has tasked a consultant with reviewing report creation software to ensure seamless integration with existing database systems for regulatory compliance and performance monitoring. This evaluation aims to select new software capable of generating plant operating reports required by regulations, distinct from the core SCADA migration tasks [Evaluation Memo] [RFQ].

**Submission Logistics**
Submissions related to this RFQ (including Standard Form SF330, "Ban-The-Box" compliance, and SLBE solicitation forms) must be emailed to ContractAdministration@tampagov.net before 2 P.M. on March 19, 2026 [RFQ].

### Procurement And Commercial Requirements
**Procurement And Commercial Requirements Summary**

The City of Tampa's Request for Qualifications (RFQ) for the Howard F. Curren Advanced Water Treatment Plant (HFC AWTP) SCADA migration project establishes specific submission protocols, legal compliance mandates, and commercial constraints distinct from technical evaluation criteria or design standards. All proposals must be submitted as a PDF file via email to `ContractAdministration@tampagov.net` no later than 2:00 P.M. on March 19, 2026; however, the City does not guarantee acknowledgement for same-day submissions received by this deadline [RFQ]. Firms are responsible for confirming receipt of their proposals and must adhere to Florida Statute Section 287.055(10) regarding proposal reuse, as well as exemptions concerning internal building plans per Sections 119.071(3), 119.07(1), and Article I, Section 24(a) of the Florida Constitution [RFQ].

**Mandatory Forms and Good Faith Efforts (GFE)**
To avoid being deemed non-responsive, firms must complete, sign, and submit specific forms: DMI Form 10 (Solicited Subcontractors), DMI Form 20 (Utilized Subcontractors), and DMI Form 50 (Good Faith Effort Compliance Plan) [RFQ]. Failure to provide these renders the bid non-responsive. The GFE plan must document outreach efforts via email, fax, letters, or phone calls for all firms on the SLBE Goal Setting List. Proof of certification from Small Local Business Enterprises (SLBE) is required alongside these forms. All subcontractors projected to be utilized must be included in DMI Form 20 regardless of their ownership structure or size, while solicited and unsolicited quotation recipients are listed on DMI Form 10. The prime contractor remains responsible for Good Faith Efforts even if they possess the ability to perform work themselves; other subcontracting opportunities should still be explored to meet participation goals [Other Source].

**Evaluation Criteria Distinction and Technical Scope**
While technical requirements such as PLC hardware longevity, VT SCADA compatibility, software cost competitiveness, and customer service accessibility are detailed in Evaluation Memos, these represent scoring criteria rather than binding RFQ submission constraints. Similarly, documentation standards for change management (e.g., date codes, programmer initials) found in the SCADA Standards Volume 2 apply to project execution but do not constitute immediate procurement requirements unless explicitly referenced as such [Evaluation Memo].

The commercial landscape centers on migrating monitoring capabilities to VTScada software while adhering to specific network diagrams, control system programming needs, and regulatory reporting requirements. The City aims to select a new report creation platform that integrates seamlessly with existing databases to ensure efficient plant performance identification and compliance. Evaluation memos highlight significant cost disparities among platforms: the PI System is identified as the highest-cost option ($174k/year) due to its licensing structure, whereas XLReport offers a lower recurring subscription model (~$5,850/year). Licensing constraints are noted as restrictive for some options regarding tag and user counts. Technical standards mandate that source protection (locking proprietary code) be prohibited, access to network hosts require authenticated encrypted connections, and remote sites must be polled from central locations without direct database interaction by operators [Evaluation Memo].

### Scope of Work: Technical Standards, System Architecture, Hardware Specifications, and Procedural Requirements

The project scope for the City of Tampa's migration from legacy HSQ software to VTScada at facilities such as the Howard F. Curren Advanced Wastewater Treatment Plant (HFC AWTP) establishes a comprehensive framework integrating established SCADA standards with specific project constraints [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf]. Managed by Hazen and Sawyer under City oversight, the work encompasses documenting network diagrams in Microsoft Visio/PDF, programming control systems using Studio 5000 Logix Designer (Version 36 or latest), selecting new hardware that meets regulatory needs, evaluating fiber optic replacement alternatives with cost estimates, preparing signed construction plans [RFQ], and preserving historical HSQ data from January 2000 onward within the system.

**System Architecture and Communications**
The design team must coordinate closely with process integrators and City staff for VTScada software development, graphics creation, tag programming, and troubleshooting training. The data acquisition infrastructure mandates adherence to strict redundancy requirements; specifically, at least two dedicated data acquisition servers configured as primary/backup pairs are required for collections/distributions, wastewater operations, and water operations, while separate stand-alone servers are mandated for these roles to ensure system resilience [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf], [SCADA Standard]. An industrial DMZ acts as the sole communication buffer between business networks and PCS/SCADA systems. Architectures utilizing dual-homed hosts, dial-up modems, cellular routers, or remote network connections are prohibited; updates must be performed locally on a City-provided laptop to ensure consistent software versioning [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf], [SCADA Standard]. Controllers must utilize non-volatile memory (NVM) capable of withstanding environmental conditions without relying on continuous power, while all communications are configured in VTScada using appropriate drivers to match specific protocols. The existing surveillance equipment inventory includes specific Sony Ipela models deployed in chlorine rooms, railcars, switchgear areas, and gates, complemented by Cambium Canopy devices utilized for railcar transmission [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf].

**Design Criteria & Documentation Deliverables**
Process and Instrumentation Diagrams (P&IDs) must be developed using AutoCAD Plant 3D, 2022, or Smart P&ID software to ensure accurate I/O lists. Tagging protocols distinguish between process equipment and instrumentation tags, with legends provided in Appendix C of relevant documentation [Background Reference], [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf]. Key decisions include assigning building numbers 054 (Sludge Storage Tanks Control Building), 058 (Blower Building), and new structures as 065/066 (*Workshop Note*) [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf].

**Programming Logic, Languages, and Nomenclature**
Development must utilize Ladder Diagrams (LD) or Function Block Diagrams (FBD); other languages like Instruction List are not permitted without a formal deviation request [SCADA Standard], [Background Reference]. LD is preferred for complex Boolean logic, interlocks, sequencing, and I/O control. While function blocks may be used within rung logic, the system enforces a strict contract regarding data types (Booleans, integers, reals) accessible via AOI interfaces using EasyBuilder Pro V6 with specific function blocks defined for Modbus communication: `RAC_OPRNETMODBUSTCPMASTER` for sending data and `RAC_OPRNETMODBUSTCPCLIENT`.

### Controls And Integration Requirements
**Controls and Integration Requirements Summary**

The City of Tampa's SCADA and Process Control System (PCS) framework establishes strict controls for change management, programming languages, security, hardware integration, and network architecture to ensure consistent reliability across all plants. The system design mandates a robust VTScada 12.1.58 architecture from Trihedral Engineering as the foundation for all new HMI screen development; consequently, all graphical elements must be created using the latest available City-provided ChangeSet file to ensure application recreation capability [SCADA Standard]. Redundancy is achieved through at least two dedicated, stand-alone servers configured as primary and backup roles specifically for data acquisition across collections/distributions, waste water operations, and water operations.

**Network Architecture and Security Zones**
The system architecture mandates strict network segmentation to ensure security and autonomy. An industrial DMZ must act as a buffer between business networks (e.g., PIN) and PCS/SCADA systems, serving as the sole communication bridge; dual-homed hosts are prohibited [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf]. Network architecture must maintain disconnection points between security zones to allow for isolation while preserving limited operational continuity. Remote sites shall operate autonomously without dependency on other plant PCs. Authentication is centrally managed via domain-level services (e.g., Active Directory), with local authentication explicitly noted where used exclusively, and a dedicated solution required for the SCADA system [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf]. Physical access controls require keyed locks for control rooms/server areas, disabling unused router/switch ports except during configuration/testing. Remote desktop protocols (RDP) or third-party tools lacking domain authentication, accounting, encryption, and strong security features are prohibited. Dial-up modems and cellular routers are also restricted [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf].

**Communication Protocols and Hardware Integration**
Control I/O signals interfacing with vendor-supplied PLCs must be hardwired, distinct from the overall system PLCs provided by the integrator. Communication protocols between remote controllers and the supervisory system shall strictly adhere to DNP3 (as specified by DNP.ORG) on remote sites, utilizing appropriate transport layers like LAN or serial modems while prohibiting proprietary protocols; polling must be event-driven rather than static value requests where possible [SCADA Standard]. Hardware specifications require industrial-grade HMI displays capable of withstanding harsh environments, connected via shielded CAT6 cable rated for 480V exposure where applicable. Power resilience is addressed through the use of APC UPS units without direct ethernet connections and equipped with bypass switches to facilitate easy changeouts; relays must be installed on UPS outputs to trigger alarms when panels operate solely on backup power [Background Reference]. Regarding equipment selection for PLC upgrades at the HFC AWTP, industry reviews identify GE (RX3i series), Rockwell (Allen Bradley Logix series), and Schneider (Modicon M540/580) as viable options with significant municipal wastewater market share. While these manufacturers were selected for further evaluation in an Evaluation Memo [Evaluation Memo], the specific decision to select one over another remains distinct from binding SCADA standards which focus on software compatibility at this stage. Field investigations have verified routing details noting that PLC 19 in the Main Pump Station connects directly via CAT6 cable rather than fiber, while eleven main trunk fiber cables are landed at a patch panel for process connections [Other Source/Field Investigation].

**Programming Logic and Development Environment**
PLC programming is mandated to use Studio 5000 Logix Designer Version 36 or later; the main routine must govern program flow using LD language (or FBDs) for Boolean equations, jump subroutines (

### Network And Electrical Infrastructure
**Network and Electrical Infrastructure Summary**

The project involves a SCADA migration and fiber replacement design for the Howard F. Curren Advanced Wastewater Treatment Plant (HFC AWTP), governed by specific hardware, network routing, and HMI standards while adhering to Florida public records exemptions regarding building plans [RFQ]. The Network and Electrical Infrastructure category addresses legacy hardware risks, data historian architecture, security patching protocols, and troubleshooting display methodologies within a SCADA environment. Specific evaluation memos highlight the presence of mixed PLC architectures across various buildings: Building 10 utilizes GE 90-30 legacy systems for RIO units 1 and 2 as well as unit 43, while Buildings 5, 59, 78, 30, and 25 employ active GE PACSystems Rx3i controllers (PLC-4, PLC-37, PLC-39, PLC-33, and PLC-40 respectively), with Building 25 also containing legacy RIO units for the Alkalinity process [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf].

**Hardware and Power Requirements:**
*   **PLC Architecture:** The system utilizes independent vendor PLCs for individual equipment systems that interface with integrator-provided overall system PLCs. Signals must be hardwired for all control I/O, and new PLCs are permitted to connect directly to the plant fiber network without remote telemetry units [Background Reference].
*   **Power Supply:** Uninterruptible power supplies (UPS) shall specifically use APC models that lack ethernet connections but include a bypass switch for easy change-out. Relays on UPS outputs must provide alarms when panels operate on backup power, and spare capacity requirements are set at 20% total with 10% installed [Background Reference].
*   **Standard Components:** Control panel components follow specific model standards (e.g., GE Rx3i CPUs) to ensure consistency across the application.

**Network Infrastructure and Routing:**
*   **Fiber vs. Copper Verification:** Field investigations confirmed that while fiber is used for main trunk cables landed at patch panels in the Main Pump Station, PLC 19 connects directly via CAT6 cable without using fiber. Existing infrastructure includes a conduit beneath the network room with limited space; replacing old fibers requires parallel installation to maintain functionality [Background Reference].
*   **Network Topology:** The facility utilizes distinct networks (e.g., Base 7 PC39328, COT Network) color-coded and zoned for specific functions like camera monitoring and server rooms. PLCs such as PLC 1, 9, and 18 act as critical hubs directing communications back to the main switch; their failure impacts connected units [Background Reference].
*   **Installation Constraints:** During new fiber installation, existing cables must remain in place. Field investigations are recommended during design phases to verify that new fibers can be installed within the existing infrastructure without interruption [Background Reference].

**Data Historian and Security Standards:**
Regarding infrastructure standards, SCADA Volume 3 mandates that historian data resides on a SQL-based server located in the PIN(DMZ) network to receive critical compliance information from acquisition servers; notably, there are no established City-wide standards for analog value logging frequency or deadbands, requiring project-specific review and approval [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf]. Security requirements outlined in SCADA Volume 1 dictate the maintenance of a patch management system within the PIN to ensure production operating systems receive vendor updates via an automated method using the City's business network and internet connection, alongside anti-virus definition updates for PCS/SCADA systems [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf].

### Deliverables And Commissioning
**Deliverables And Commissioning Summary**

The project scope requires migrating monitoring and control capabilities to VTScada software, which includes documenting SCADA network diagrams and control system programming [RFQ]. The firm must assist in selecting a new software system capable of generating plant operating reports for regulatory compliance and performance monitoring [RFQ], while also developing alternatives for replacing the fiber optic cable network with signed and sealed construction plans and specifications [RFQ].

**Hardware and System Standards:**
*   **Workstations:** Computer workstations shall be provided in key buildings to allow full control and monitoring of the entire plant from each location; existing stations are not to be replaced. All programmers must adhere to unified hardware and software standards, with independent vendor PLCs interfacing via hardwired signals for all I/O [Background Reference].
*   **Documentation Requirements:** Implementers are responsible for updating City SCADA & PCS documentation following changes, specifically including Process Control Descriptions (PCD), alarm/historian databases, P&IDs, loop diagrams, panel drawings, standard object libraries, reports, historian change logs, and network diagrams [SCADA Standard].
*   **Logic and Graphics Standards:** Ladder logic rungs must include comments describing their purpose; the first rung of a routine should summarize overall function. Digital values in HMI graphics must be shown in bold blue with simple descriptions within rectangles, consistent with Section 8.0 on alarming iconography [SCADA Standard].
*   **Task and File Organization:** FBD sheets are limited to sizes printable on an 11" x 17" sheet. Only two task types—continuous and periodic—are permitted for the City system; other types will not be employed [SCADA Standard].

**Specific Deliverable Constraints (Workshop Notes):**
*   **Instrument Tagging:** P&ID instrument tags must use standard ISA format bubbles but incorporate building letter codes alongside numbers to designate process areas. This specific tagging convention was noted as missing on previous dewater project P&IDs, requiring updates for those sheets [Workshop Note].

**Legal and Administrative Context (RFQ):**
*   The RFQ is subject to Florida Statutes Section 287.055(10) regarding reuse of information. Firms must confirm familiarity with exemptions under Sections 119.071(3) and relevant constitutional articles concerning building plans, blueprints, and schematic drawings that depict internal layouts or structural elements [RFQ].

### Qualifications And Staffing
The **Qualifications and Staffing** category primarily consists of administrative instructions regarding firm submission types (Corporation, LLC, Individual) and legal compliance with Florida Statutes Sections 287.055(10), 119.071(3), and Section 24(a), Art. I of the Florida Constitution concerning "Exempt Plans," as detailed in the RFQ Transmittal Memorandum for Project 26-D-00017 (HFC AWTP SCADA Migration). While a specific requirement regarding controller object references, HMI naming conventions matching device tags, and adherence to the City's *SCADA & PCS Standards and Conventions Manual Volume 2* is present in the evidence, this information pertains strictly to technical system design rather than staff qualifications or staffing levels. Consequently, no substantive claims regarding personnel experience, specific job roles, or staffing ratios are supported by the provided text; the category content is limited to these submission format and statutory compliance constraints found within the RFQ documents (26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf).

### Risks And Clarifications
**Risks and Clarifications Summary**

The project involves specific cybersecurity risks and operational constraints regarding software selection, firmware updates, and network policies. DreamReport is highlighted for its advantages in NIST certification for cybersecurity and 21 CFR part 11 compliance, which ensures report reliability and integration capabilities with HMI/PLC systems [Evaluation Memo]. However, strict adherence to City policy is required; all devices must be managed by these policies, unencrypted communication (e.g., email) faces restrictions, and personnel must acknowledge cybersecurity requirements before accessing the PCS/SCADA system [SCADA Standard].

Operational risks are mitigated through rigorous update procedures. Developers or responsible parties must obtain written OT contact approval for any firmware updates requiring software changes and verify hardware compatibility against manufacturer matrices prior to updating; non-compatible components require specific handling per the Request for Shutdown Form [SCADA Standard]. Additionally, new software requirements necessitate a minimum four-week lead time for security evaluation and loading by the developer before startup begins [SCADA Standard].

Clarifications regarding design criteria were addressed in workshop notes. For instance, equipment tagging formats on revised P&IDs required correction to match specific building number conventions (e.g., XXXYYY ZZZ-AAA-BB), distinguishing these open items from binding standards or RFQ requirements [Workshop Note]. While tag naming codes exist for site locations and instruments, the documentation acknowledges that unaddressed needs may require submitting suggested additions to the City for consideration and approval before use [SCADA Standard].

### Executive Summary
**Executive Summary: SCADA Migration Project (HFC AWTP)**

The City of Tampa is initiating a comprehensive migration at the Howard F. Curren Advanced Water Treatment Plant (AWTP) to transition from legacy HSQ software to VTScada, ensuring continuous operation for its 96 mgd sewage treatment facility while upgrading decades-old infrastructure [RFQ]. Managed by Hazen and Sawyer under City oversight, this initiative aligns with established SCADA standards and addresses specific procurement protocols required by Florida Statute.

**Technical Scope and Architecture**
The project mandates a robust VTScada 12.1.58 architecture featuring redundant primary/backup data acquisition servers for all operations [SCADA Standard]. System design must strictly adhere to network segmentation policies, utilizing an industrial DMZ as the sole bridge between business networks (PIN) and PCS systems while prohibiting dual-homed hosts [RFQ; SCADA Standard]. Technical deliverables include documenting legacy GE PLC architectures (including 90-30 and PACSystems Rx3i units), preserving historical data from January 2000, and developing fiber optic replacement alternatives with signed construction plans [Background Reference; RFQ]. All hardware must interface via hardwired signals for control I/O, utilizing existing workstations where possible to maintain unified standards [Deliverables And Commissioning Summary].

**Compliance, Security, and Procurement Constraints**
Firms are subject to strict commercial constraints distinct from technical evaluation criteria. Proposals must be submitted as PDFs by March 19, 2026, accompanied mandatory Good Faith Effort (GFE) forms documenting subcontractor outreach [RFQ]. Cybersecurity is a critical risk mitigation factor; all devices require management under City policy with written OT contact approval for firmware updates and a minimum four-week lead time for security evaluation before startup begins [Risks And Clarifications Summary]. Additionally, the project leverages DreamReport software advantages regarding NIST certification and 21 CFR part 11 compliance to ensure report reliability [Evaluation Memo], while adhering to Florida public records exemptions concerning internal building plans [RFQ].

### Key Findings
* **Mandatory Submission Requirements:** Proposers must email a PDF submission containing SF330 forms, "Ban-The-Box" compliance data, SLBE utilization documentation (DMI 10,20,& 50), and the amount of City Work to ContractAdministration@tampagov.net before 2 P.M. on March 19, 2026; late submissions may not receive acknowledgement [Source: Procurement And Commercial Requirements].
* **Software Migration & Reporting Scope:** The project requires migrating monitoring/control capabilities to VTScada software and implementing a new system capable of generating regulatory plant operating reports; existing computer workstations will remain in place while independent vendor PLCs interface with integrator-provided overall system PLCs [Source: Deliverables And Commissioning].
* **Programming Language Constraints:** All control programming must strictly adhere to IEC 61131-3 standards, utilizing only Ladder Logic (LD) and Function Block Diagram (FBD); other languages such as Instruction List or Sequential Function Flowchart are not allowable [Source: Risks And Clarifications].
* **Cybersecurity & Compliance Standards:** New software must be submitted to the City with a minimum four-week lead time for security evaluation, and preferred solutions like DreamReport offer NIST certification which supports 21 CFR part 11 compliance requirements; all hardware/software programmers must follow unified standards [Source: Risks And Clarifications].
* **HMI Design Guidelines:** All new HMI objects and displays must strictly follow ISA-101 recommendations to maintain alignment with the City's Standard Object Library for effective process automation applications [Source: Network And Electrical Infrastructure].
* **Naming Convention Protocol:** A one-to-one relationship between controller tags and HMI visualized elements is required, ensuring tag names in the HMI are identical to those originating or terminating in the device per SCADA & PCS Standards Volume 2 [Source: Qualifications And Staffing].

### Next Actions
**Next Actions:**

* **Submit RFQ Transmittal Package:** Email a PDF package containing the amount of City Work calculation (5 pts), Standard Form SF330 (5 pts), "Ban-The-Box" documentation (5 pts), and SLBE Solicitation/Utilization Forms DMI 10, 20, & 50 (15 pts) to `ContractAdministration@tampagov.net` before **2 P.M., March 19, 2026**. *(Source: Category: Procurement And Commercial Requirements / Qualifications And Staffing)*
* **Verify Submission Receipt:** Confirm receipt of the submitted PDF file with Contract Administration immediately after submission, as acknowledgements may be delayed or absent for same-day submissions. *(Source: Category: Procurement And Commercial Requirements)*
* **Prepare Good Faith Effort (GFE) Documentation:** Compile proof of certification and completed DMI 10, 20, & 50 Forms to document inclusion of City-certified SLBE firms in all Phase 1 preconstruction design services. *(Source: Category: Scope Of Work)*
* **Review Hardware Standards for PLCs:** Ensure independent vendor PLC hardware plans adhere to the requirement that signals to controls are hardwired and that workstations allow full plant monitoring without replacing existing units. *(Source: Category: Controls And Integration Requirements)*
* **Validate HMI Design against ISA 101:** Confirm all new objects, displays, and graphics symbols in the proposed system strictly follow current ISA 101 recommendations to maintain industry standards. *(Source: Category: Network And Electrical Infrastructure)*
* **Check Software Security Compliance:** Verify that any proposed new software (e.g., DreamReport) is NIST certified for cybersecurity and capable of meeting 21 CFR part 11 compliance requirements before submission. *(Source: Category: Risks And Clarifications)*
* **Confirm Programming Language Usage:** Ensure all control system programming utilizes only Ladder Logic (LD) or Function Block Diagram (FBD), excluding Instruction List, Sequential Function Flowchart, and other non-approved languages per IEC 61131-3. *(Source: Category: Risks And Clarifications)*
* **Plan Software Security Evaluation Timeline:** Schedule a minimum of four weeks lead time to submit new software requirements for City security evaluation and loading prior to start-up. *(Source: Category: Risks And Clarifications)*

**Procurement And Commercial Requirements Summary**

The City of Tampa's Request for Qualifications (RFQ) for the Howard F. Curren Advanced Water Treatment Plant (HFC AWTP) SCADA migration project establishes specific submission protocols, legal compliance mandates, and commercial constraints distinct from technical evaluation criteria or design standards. All proposals must be submitted as a PDF file via email to `ContractAdministration@tampagov.net` no later than 2:00 P.M. on March 19, 2026; however, the City does not guarantee acknowledgement for same-day submissions received by this deadline [RFQ]. Firms are responsible for confirming receipt of their proposals and must adhere to Florida Statute Section 287.055(10) regarding proposal reuse, as well as exemptions concerning internal building plans per Sections 119.071(3), 119.07(1), and Article I, Section 24(a) of the Florida Constitution [RFQ].

**Mandatory Forms and Good Faith Efforts (GFE)**
To avoid being deemed non-responsive, firms must complete, sign, and submit specific forms: DMI Form 10 (Solicited Subcontractors), DMI Form 20 (Utilized Subcontractors), and DMI Form 50 (Good Faith Effort Compliance Plan) [RFQ]. Failure to provide these renders the bid non-responsive. The GFE plan must document outreach efforts via email, fax, letters, or phone calls for all firms on the SLBE Goal Setting List. Proof of certification from Small Local Business Enterprises (SLBE) is required alongside these forms. All subcontractors projected to be utilized must be included in DMI Form 20 regardless of their ownership structure or size, while solicited and unsolicited quotation recipients are listed on DMI Form 10. The prime contractor remains responsible for Good Faith Efforts even if they possess the ability to perform work themselves; other subcontracting opportunities should still be explored to meet participation goals [Other Source].

**Evaluation Criteria Distinction and Technical Scope**
While technical requirements such as PLC hardware longevity, VT SCADA compatibility, software cost competitiveness, and customer service accessibility are detailed in Evaluation Memos, these represent scoring criteria rather than binding RFQ submission constraints. Similarly, documentation standards for change management (e.g., date codes, programmer initials) found in the SCADA Standards Volume 2 apply to project execution but do not constitute immediate procurement requirements unless explicitly referenced as such [Evaluation Memo].

The commercial landscape centers on migrating monitoring capabilities to VTScada software while adhering to specific network diagrams, control system programming needs, and regulatory reporting requirements. The City aims to select a new report creation platform that integrates seamlessly with existing databases to ensure efficient plant performance identification and compliance. Evaluation memos highlight significant cost disparities among platforms: the PI System is identified as the highest-cost option ($174k/year) due to its licensing structure, whereas XLReport offers a lower recurring subscription model (~$5,850/year). Licensing constraints are noted as restrictive for some options regarding tag and user counts. Technical standards mandate that source protection (locking proprietary code) be prohibited, access to network hosts require authenticated encrypted connections, and remote sites must be polled from central locations without direct database interaction by operators [Evaluation Memo].

### Scope of Work: Technical Standards, System Architecture, Hardware Specifications, and Procedural Requirements

The project scope for the City of Tampa's migration from legacy HSQ software to VTScada at facilities such as the Howard F. Curren Advanced Wastewater Treatment Plant (HFC AWTP) establishes a comprehensive framework integrating established SCADA standards with specific project constraints [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf]. Managed by Hazen and Sawyer under City oversight, the work encompasses documenting network diagrams in Microsoft Visio/PDF, programming control systems using Studio 5000 Logix Designer (Version 36 or latest), selecting new hardware that meets regulatory needs, evaluating fiber optic replacement alternatives with cost estimates, preparing signed construction plans [RFQ], and preserving historical HSQ data from January 2000 onward within the system.

**System Architecture and Communications**
The design team must coordinate closely with process integrators and City staff for VTScada software development, graphics creation, tag programming, and troubleshooting training. The data acquisition infrastructure mandates adherence to strict redundancy requirements; specifically, at least two dedicated data acquisition servers configured as primary/backup pairs are required for collections/distributions, wastewater operations, and water operations, while separate stand-alone servers are mandated for these roles to ensure system resilience [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf], [SCADA Standard]. An industrial DMZ acts as the sole communication buffer between business networks and PCS/SCADA systems. Architectures utilizing dual-homed hosts, dial-up modems, cellular routers, or remote network connections are prohibited; updates must be performed locally on a City-provided laptop to ensure consistent software versioning [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf], [SCADA Standard]. Controllers must utilize non-volatile memory (NVM) capable of withstanding environmental conditions without relying on continuous power, while all communications are configured in VTScada using appropriate drivers to match specific protocols. The existing surveillance equipment inventory includes specific Sony Ipela models deployed in chlorine rooms, railcars, switchgear areas, and gates, complemented by Cambium Canopy devices utilized for railcar transmission [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf].

**Design Criteria & Documentation Deliverables**
Process and Instrumentation Diagrams (P&IDs) must be developed using AutoCAD Plant 3D, 2022, or Smart P&ID software to ensure accurate I/O lists. Tagging protocols distinguish between process equipment and instrumentation tags, with legends provided in Appendix C of relevant documentation [Background Reference], [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf]. Key decisions include assigning building numbers 054 (Sludge Storage Tanks Control Building), 058 (Blower Building), and new structures as 065/066 (*Workshop Note*) [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf].

**Programming Logic, Languages, and Nomenclature**
Development must utilize Ladder Diagrams (LD) or Function Block Diagrams (FBD); other languages like Instruction List are not permitted without a formal deviation request [SCADA Standard], [Background Reference]. LD is preferred for complex Boolean logic, interlocks, sequencing, and I/O control. While function blocks may be used within rung logic, the system enforces a strict contract regarding data types (Booleans, integers, reals) accessible via AOI interfaces using EasyBuilder Pro V6 with specific function blocks defined for Modbus communication: `RAC_OPRNETMODBUSTCPMASTER` for sending data and `RAC_OPRNETMODBUSTCPCLIENT`.

### Controls And Integration Requirements
**Controls and Integration Requirements Summary**

The City of Tampa's SCADA and Process Control System (PCS) framework establishes strict controls for change management, programming languages, security, hardware integration, and network architecture to ensure consistent reliability across all plants. The system design mandates a robust VTScada 12.1.58 architecture from Trihedral Engineering as the foundation for all new HMI screen development; consequently, all graphical elements must be created using the latest available City-provided ChangeSet file to ensure application recreation capability [SCADA Standard]. Redundancy is achieved through at least two dedicated, stand-alone servers configured as primary and backup roles specifically for data acquisition across collections/distributions, waste water operations, and water operations.

**Network Architecture and Security Zones**
The system architecture mandates strict network segmentation to ensure security and autonomy. An industrial DMZ must act as a buffer between business networks (e.g., PIN) and PCS/SCADA systems, serving as the sole communication bridge; dual-homed hosts are prohibited [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf]. Network architecture must maintain disconnection points between security zones to allow for isolation while preserving limited operational continuity. Remote sites shall operate autonomously without dependency on other plant PCs. Authentication is centrally managed via domain-level services (e.g., Active Directory), with local authentication explicitly noted where used exclusively, and a dedicated solution required for the SCADA system [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf]. Physical access controls require keyed locks for control rooms/server areas, disabling unused router/switch ports except during configuration/testing. Remote desktop protocols (RDP) or third-party tools lacking domain authentication, accounting, encryption, and strong security features are prohibited. Dial-up modems and cellular routers are also restricted [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf].

**Communication Protocols and Hardware Integration**
Control I/O signals interfacing with vendor-supplied PLCs must be hardwired, distinct from the overall system PLCs provided by the integrator. Communication protocols between remote controllers and the supervisory system shall strictly adhere to DNP3 (as specified by DNP.ORG) on remote sites, utilizing appropriate transport layers like LAN or serial modems while prohibiting proprietary protocols; polling must be event-driven rather than static value requests where possible [SCADA Standard]. Hardware specifications require industrial-grade HMI displays capable of withstanding harsh environments, connected via shielded CAT6 cable rated for 480V exposure where applicable. Power resilience is addressed through the use of APC UPS units without direct ethernet connections and equipped with bypass switches to facilitate easy changeouts; relays must be installed on UPS outputs to trigger alarms when panels operate solely on backup power [Background Reference]. Regarding equipment selection for PLC upgrades at the HFC AWTP, industry reviews identify GE (RX3i series), Rockwell (Allen Bradley Logix series), and Schneider (Modicon M540/580) as viable options with significant municipal wastewater market share. While these manufacturers were selected for further evaluation in an Evaluation Memo [Evaluation Memo], the specific decision to select one over another remains distinct from binding SCADA standards which focus on software compatibility at this stage. Field investigations have verified routing details noting that PLC 19 in the Main Pump Station connects directly via CAT6 cable rather than fiber, while eleven main trunk fiber cables are landed at a patch panel for process connections [Other Source/Field Investigation].
