### Network And Electrical Infrastructure
**Network and Electrical Infrastructure Summary**

The project involves a SCADA migration and fiber replacement design for the Howard F. Curren Advanced Wastewater Treatment Plant (HFC AWTP), governed by specific hardware, network routing, and HMI standards while adhering to Florida public records exemptions regarding building plans [RFQ]. The Network and Electrical Infrastructure category addresses legacy hardware risks, data historian architecture, security patching protocols, and troubleshooting display methodologies within a SCADA environment. Specific evaluation memos highlight the presence of mixed PLC architectures across various buildings: Building 10 utilizes GE 90-30 legacy systems for RIO units 1 and 2 as well as unit 43, while Buildings 5, 59, 78, 30, and 25 employ active GE PACSystems Rx3i controllers (PLC-4, PLC-37, PLC-39, PLC-33, and PLC-40 respectively), with Building 25 also containing legacy RIO units for the Alkalinity process [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf].

**Hardware and Power Requirements:**
* **PLC Architecture:** The system utilizes independent vendor PLCs for individual equipment systems that interface with integrator-provided overall system PLCs. Signals must be hardwired for all control I/O, and new PLCs are permitted to connect directly to the plant fiber network without remote telemetry units [Background Reference].
* **Power Supply:** Uninterruptible power supplies (UPS) shall specifically use APC models that lack ethernet connections but include a bypass switch for easy change-out. Relays on UPS outputs must provide alarms when panels operate on backup power, and spare capacity requirements are set at 20% total with 10% installed [Background Reference].
* **Standard Components:** Control panel components follow specific model standards (e.g., GE Rx3i CPUs) to ensure consistency across the application.

**Network Infrastructure and Routing:**
* **Fiber vs. Copper Verification:** Field investigations confirmed that while fiber is used for main trunk cables landed at patch panels in the Main Pump Station, PLC 19 connects directly via CAT6 cable without using fiber. Existing infrastructure includes a conduit beneath the network room with limited space; replacing old fibers requires parallel installation to maintain functionality [Background Reference].
* **Network Topology:** The facility utilizes distinct networks (e.g., Base 7 PC39328, COT Network) color-coded and zoned for specific functions like camera monitoring and server rooms. PLCs such as PLC 1, 9, and 18 act as critical hubs directing communications back to the main switch; their failure impacts connected units [Background Reference].
* **Installation Constraints:** During new fiber installation, existing cables must remain in place. Field investigations are recommended during design phases to verify that new fibers can be installed within the existing infrastructure without interruption [Background Reference].

**Data Historian and Security Standards:**
Regarding infrastructure standards, SCADA Volume 3 mandates that historian data resides on a SQL-based server located in the PIN(DMZ) network to receive critical compliance information from acquisition servers; notably, there are no established City-wide standards for analog value logging frequency or deadbands, requiring project-specific review and approval [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf]. Security requirements outlined in SCADA Volume 1 dictate the maintenance of a patch management system within the PIN to ensure production operating systems receive vendor updates via an automated method using the City's business network and internet connection, alongside anti-virus definition updates for PCS/SCADA systems [26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf].

### Deliverables And Commissioning
**Deliverables And Commissioning Summary**

The project scope requires migrating monitoring and control capabilities to VTScada software, which includes documenting SCADA network diagrams and control system programming [RFQ]. The firm must assist in selecting a new software system capable of generating plant operating reports for regulatory compliance and performance monitoring [RFQ], while also developing alternatives for replacing the fiber optic cable network with signed and sealed construction plans and specifications [RFQ].

**Hardware and System Standards:**
* **Workstations:** Computer workstations shall be provided in key buildings to allow full control and monitoring of the entire plant from each location; existing stations are not to be replaced. All programmers must adhere to unified hardware and software standards, with independent vendor PLCs interfacing via hardwired signals for all I/O [Background Reference].
* **Documentation Requirements:** Implementers are responsible for updating City SCADA & PCS documentation following changes, specifically including Process Control Descriptions (PCD), alarm/historian databases, P&IDs, loop diagrams, panel drawings, standard object libraries, reports, historian change logs, and network diagrams [SCADA Standard].
* **Logic and Graphics Standards:** Ladder logic rungs must include comments describing their purpose; the first rung of a routine should summarize overall function. Digital values in HMI graphics must be shown in bold blue with simple descriptions within rectangles, consistent with Section 8.0 on alarming iconography [SCADA Standard].
* **Task and File Organization:** FBD sheets are limited to sizes printable on an 11" x 17" sheet. Only two task types—continuous and periodic—are permitted for the City system; other types will not be employed [SCADA Standard].

**Specific Deliverable Constraints (Workshop Notes):**
* **Instrument Tagging:** P&ID instrument tags must use standard ISA format bubbles but incorporate building letter codes alongside numbers to designate process areas. This specific tagging convention was noted as missing on previous dewater project P&IDs, requiring updates for those sheets [Workshop Note].

**Legal and Administrative Context (RFQ):**
* The RFQ is subject to Florida Statutes Section 287.055(10) regarding reuse of information. Firms must confirm familiarity with exemptions under Sections 119.071(3) and relevant constitutional articles concerning building plans, blueprints, and schematic drawings that depict internal layouts or structural elements [RFQ].

### Qualifications And Staffing
The **Qualifications and Staffing** category primarily consists of administrative instructions regarding firm submission types (Corporation, LLC, Individual) and legal compliance with Florida Statutes Sections 287.055(10), 119.071(3), and Section 24(a), Art. I of the Florida Constitution concerning "Exempt Plans," as detailed in the RFQ Transmittal Memorandum for Project 26-D-00017 (HFC AWTP SCADA Migration). While a specific requirement regarding controller object references, HMI naming conventions matching device tags, and adherence to the City's *SCADA & PCS Standards and Conventions Manual Volume 2* is present in the evidence, this information pertains strictly to technical system design rather than staff qualifications or staffing levels. Consequently, no substantive claims regarding personnel experience, specific job roles, or staffing ratios are supported by the provided text; the category content is limited to these submission format and statutory compliance constraints found within the RFQ documents (26-d-00017-rfqupost.pdf).

### Risks And Clarifications
**Risks and Clarifications Summary**

The project involves specific cybersecurity risks and operational constraints regarding software selection, firmware updates, and network policies. DreamReport is highlighted for its advantages in NIST certification for cybersecurity and 21 CFR part 11 compliance, which ensures report reliability and integration capabilities with HMI/PLC systems [Evaluation Memo]. However, strict adherence to City policy is required; all devices must be managed by these policies, unencrypted communication (e.g., email) faces restrictions, and personnel must acknowledge cybersecurity requirements before accessing the PCS/SCADA system [SCADA Standard].

Operational risks are mitigated through rigorous update procedures. Developers or responsible parties must obtain written OT contact approval for any firmware updates requiring software changes and verify hardware compatibility against manufacturer matrices prior to updating; non-compatible components require specific handling per the Request for Shutdown Form [SCADA Standard]. Additionally, new software requirements necessitate a minimum four-week lead time for security evaluation and loading by the developer before startup begins [SCADA Standard].

Clarifications regarding design criteria were addressed in workshop notes. For instance, equipment tagging formats on revised P&IDs required correction to match specific building number conventions (e.g., XXXYYY ZZZ-AAA-BB), distinguishing these open items from binding standards or RFQ requirements [Workshop Note]. While tag naming codes exist for site locations and instruments, the documentation acknowledges that unaddressed needs may require submitting suggested additions to the City for consideration and approval before use [SCADA Standard].

### Executive Summary
**Executive Summary: SCADA Migration Project (HFC AWTP)**

The City of Tampa is initiating a comprehensive migration at the Howard F. Curren Advanced Water Treatment Plant (AWTP) to transition from legacy HSQ software to VTScada, ensuring continuous operation for its 96 mgd sewage treatment facility while upgrading decades-old infrastructure [RFQ]. Managed by Hazen and Sawyer under City oversight, this initiative aligns with established SCADA standards and addresses specific procurement protocols required by Florida Statute.

**Technical Scope and Architecture**
The project mandates a robust VTScada 12.1.58 architecture featuring redundant primary/backup data acquisition servers for all operations [SCADA Standard]. System design must strictly adhere to network segmentation policies, utilizing an industrial DMZ as the sole bridge between business networks (PIN) and PCS systems while prohibiting dual-homed hosts [RFQ; SCADA Standard]. Technical deliverables include documenting legacy GE PLC architectures (including 90-30 and PACSystems Rx3i units), preserving historical data from January 2000, and developing fiber optic replacement alternatives with signed construction plans [Background Reference; RFQ]. All hardware must interface via hardwired signals for control I/O, utilizing existing workstations where possible to maintain unified standards [Deliverables And Commissioning Summary].

**Compliance, Security, and Procurement Constraints**
Firms are subject to strict commercial constraints distinct from technical evaluation criteria. Proposals must be submitted as PDFs by March 19, 2026, accompanied mandatory Good Faith Effort (GFE) forms documenting subcontractor outreach [RFQ]. Cybersecurity is a critical risk mitigation factor; all devices require management under City policy with written OT contact approval for firmware updates and a minimum four-week lead time for security evaluation before startup begins [Risks And Clarifications Summary]. Additionally, the project leverages DreamReport software advantages regarding NIST certification and 21 CFR part 11 compliance to ensure report reliability [Evaluation Memo], while adhering to Florida public records exemptions concerning internal building plans [RFQ].

### Key Findings
* **Mandatory Submission Requirements:** Proposers must email a PDF submission containing SF330 forms, "Ban-The-Box" compliance data, SLBE utilization documentation (DMI 10,20,& 50), and the amount of City Work to ContractAdministration@tampagov.net before 2 P.M. on March 19, 2026; late submissions may not receive acknowledgement [Source: Procurement And Commercial Requirements].
* **Software Migration & Reporting Scope:** The project requires migrating monitoring/control capabilities to VTScada software and implementing a new system capable of generating regulatory plant operating reports; existing computer workstations will remain in place while independent vendor PLCs interface with integrator-provided overall system PLCs [Source: Deliverables And Commissioning].
* **Programming Language Constraints:** All control programming must strictly adhere to IEC 61131-3 standards, utilizing only Ladder Logic (LD) and Function Block Diagram (FBD); other languages such as Instruction List or Sequential Function Flowchart are not allowable [Source: Risks And Clarifications].
* **Cybersecurity & Compliance Standards:** New software must be submitted to the City with a minimum four-week lead time for security evaluation, and preferred solutions like DreamReport offer NIST certification which supports 21 CFR part 11 compliance requirements; all hardware/software programmers must follow unified standards [Source: Risks And Clarifications].
* **HMI Design Guidelines:** All new HMI objects and displays must strictly follow ISA-101 recommendations to maintain alignment with the City's Standard Object Library for effective process automation applications [Source: Network And Electrical Infrastructure].
* **Naming Convention Protocol:** A one-to-one relationship between controller tags and HMI visualized elements is required, ensuring tag names in the HMI are identical to those originating or terminating in the device per SCADA & PCS Standards Volume 2 [Source: Qualifications And Staffing].

* **Plan Software Security Evaluation Timeline:** Schedule a minimum of four weeks lead time to submit new software requirements for City security evaluation and loading prior to start-up. *(Source: Category: Risks And Clarifications)*
